title: Porosity control process of modified natural rubber foam and its application for oil sorption
abstract: Process to control pore size of the oil sorbent foam from modified natural rubber and its applications where the pore size can be controlled by agitator speed. The pore size effected to the rate of oil sorption. The density of the foam from this process was in the range of 0.28-0.56g/cm with open pore structure with pore size less than 100 microns.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. The process to control porosity of the modified natural rubber comprising: a) Preparation of rubber foam for oil sorbent i. Mix a concentrated natural rubber latex (60% dry rubber content) with vulcanizing ingredients at agitator speed of 300-1200 rpm at 22-25Â°C, steam vulcanized for 20-30 min and air dry at 30-35Â°C. b) Pore size control i. A latex compound from step a. and blowing agent was mixing speed in the range of 300 rpm to 1,200 rpm 2. The oil-based absorbent foam obtained by the process of production according to claim 1 comprising Concentrated rubber latex 100 (phr) Potassium oleate 1.5 - 2.0 (phr) Alkyl acrylate emulsion 1.0 - 2.0 (phr) Sulfur Less than 2.0 (phr) Zinc diethyldithiocarbamate Less than 1.0 (phr) Mercaptobenzothiazole Less than 1.0 (phr) Wing stay L Less than 1.0 (phr) Zinc oxide Less than 5.0 (phr) Diphenylguanidine Less than 0.7 (phr) Sodium silicofluoride 0.5 - 1.0 (phr) The oil-based absorbent foam from the process as according to claim 1. in which the density was 0.28-0.56 g/cm 3 . The oil-based absorbent foam from the process as according to claim 1. in which the characteristic was open pore diameter less than 100 micron.
